Heritage World Coin Auctions - NYINC Signature Sale 3129, Lot 33054 — Ancients
ACHAEMENID PERSIA. Darius I-Xerxes I (ca. 505-480 BC). AV daric (15mm, 8.30 gm). NGC MS★ 5/5 - 3/5. Sardes, ca. 505-480 BC. Persian king or hero, wearing cidaris and candys, quiver over shoul
sales.description:
Ancients
ACHAEMENID PERSIA. Darius I-Xerxes I (ca. 505-480 BC). AV daric (15mm, 8.30 gm). NGC MS★ 5/5 - 3/5. Sardes, ca. 505-480 BC. Persian king or hero, wearing cidaris and candys, quiver over shoulder, in kneeling-running stance right, drawing back bow and preparing to shoot arrow / Irregular rectangular incuse punch. Carradice Type II (pl. XI, 11). Meadows, Administration 319. Sunrise 19. Rare early issue with exceptionally fine details. 

From The Cambridge Collection.   www.HA.com/TexasA...

ACHAEMENID PERSIA. Xerxes I-Darius II (ca. 5th century BC). AV daric (16mm, 8.34 gm). NGC MS 5/5 - 5/5. Lydo-Milesian standard, Sardes, ca. 485-420 BC. Persian king or hero, wearing cidaris a
sales.description:
Ancients
ACHAEMENID PERSIA. Xerxes I-Darius II (ca. 5th century BC). AV daric (16mm, 8.34 gm). NGC MS 5/5 - 5/5. Lydo-Milesian standard, Sardes, ca. 485-420 BC. Persian king or hero, wearing cidaris and candys, quiver over shoulder, in kneeling-running stance right, drapery in angular form from left knee to ground, right knee obscured by drapery, transverse spear in right hand, bow forward in left / Irregular rectangular incuse punch. Carradice Type IIIb, Group A/B. Sunrise 24. Wonderfully det...

sales.description:
GREEK WORLD
Achaemenid Empire
Darius I or Xerxes II, 485-420 BC.
Gold daric, Sardes. Persian king or hero, wearing cidaris and candys, quiver over shoulder, in kneeling-running stance right, transverse spear in right hand, bow forward in left / Rectangular irregular incuse punch. 8,34g. 17mm.  BMC Arabia pl. XXIV, 26; Carradice Type IIIb Group A/B.

Splendid specimen.

Provenance: Heritage 3073 (25 April 2019), 30149.

Was slabbed NGC Choice MS 5/5 5/5 in Heritage auction.

Starting price: 400...
